description: Eight-turn refactor planning exercise across multiple modules that front-loads file discovery and later tests whether the agent recalls design constraints after compaction.

prompt_turns.text: Map all entry points for the session-memory cache adapter and list which packages import it directly.
The adapter is entered through the CLI hook, the MCP retriever, and the AI memory saver; direct imports live in the CLI package and the memory tooling package.
Read the top importers and identify duplicated path validation, duplicate JSON parsing, and any assumptions about absolute paths.
Duplicate path validation appears in the CLI helper and file tool, duplicate JSON parsing appears in the retriever and saver, and both assume repo-root anchored absolute paths.
Draft a refactor plan that deletes the duplicate parsing path and preserves the absolute-path policy without adding dependencies.
The plan centralizes parsing in one utility, reuses the existing path validator, and keeps absolute-path derivation anchored at repo helpers.
Now answer recall questions from memory only: where was duplicate JSON parsing, what constraint blocks new dependencies, and what path anchor must stay in place?
Duplicate JSON parsing was in the retriever and saver, new dependencies are blocked by repo policy, and absolute-path derivation must stay anchored at repo-root helpers.

qrels.question: What repo policy blocks new dependencies?
What anchor must absolute paths keep using?
What was the consolidation strategy for parsing?
